title: Moving money using OutboundPayment objects
excerpt: Learn how to create outbound payments to move money out of financial accounts to third parties. `OutboundPayment` objects represent push-based transfers from your financial account to a third-party external account using ACH or wire transfer, or another financial account associated with the same platform instantly using the `stripe` network. For example, if you want to send money from your financial account to your vendor’s external US bank account, you create an `OutboundPayment` to move the funds. The receiving accounts for an `OutboundPayment` are either an external bank account or another financial account. The typical transfer time for outbound payments can range from minutes (when using the Stripe network), same day, to 1-2 business days (when using the ACH network).…
